<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

An unfinished novel that was Tolstoy's original starting point before *War and Peace*. He began it in 1860, intending to tell the story of a Decembrist revolutionary returning from Siberian exile after thirty years. The project kept pulling him back in time — to the uprising of 1825, then to the Napoleonic Wars that shaped the Decembrists' generation — until it transformed entirely into *War and Peace*. The surviving fragments show the Decembrist and his wife arriving in Moscow in the 1850s, quietly idealistic in a changed world.
